description:
You are an edge-computing engineer validating how well five IoT devices behave in the field.  
    A raw log file already exists at  
    /home/user/iot_gateway/logs/device_events.log  

    1. File format of device_events.log (one single space between every field):  
       • timestamp (UTC, ISO-8601, to-the-second, suffixed with “Z”)  
       • device_id (lower-case, dash-separated; ex: “device-004”)  
       • event_type (either “DATA_UPLOAD” or “HEARTBEAT”)  
       • response_time_ms (integer, latency in milliseconds)  
       • status_code (integer, standard HTTP return code)  
       Example *structure* (do **NOT** assume these concrete numbers):  
       2024-01-31T12:58:09Z device-999 DATA_UPLOAD 123 200  

    2. Tasks you must accomplish:  
       a. Create a directory /home/user/iot_gateway/reports if it is not present.  
       b. Parse /home/user/iot_gateway/logs/device_events.log and build a CSV summary, saved exactly as  
          /home/user/iot_gateway/reports/summary.csv  
          Required column order and header (comma-separated, no extra spaces):  
          device_id,total_events,error_events,avg_response_time_ms  
          • total_events  – count of all lines for that device.  
          • error_events  – count of lines whose status_code is ≥ 400 for that device.  
          • avg_response_time_ms – arithmetic mean of response_time_ms, rounded to 2 decimal places (use 
            normal half-up rounding), with trailing zeros kept if needed (e.g. “74.50”).  
          • One line per unique device_id, sorted in lexicographical (ascending) order by device_id.  
          • The file must end with a single newline.  

       c. Produce a second text file that contains **only** those original log lines whose status_code is ≥ 400.  
          Save it as /home/user/iot_gateway/reports/error_events.log  
          Requirements:  
          • Preserve the exact order and spacing of the original lines.  
          • Do not include any additional header or footer.  
          • End the file with a single newline.  

       d. After generating both files, list the directory tree rooted at /home/user/iot_gateway using the
          `tree` command (or equivalent) so that a human reviewer can quickly verify paths.  
          Showing permissions is not required.  

    3. What the automated grader will inspect:  
       • Existence of /home/user/iot_gateway/reports/summary.csv with the exact header line and correct, 
         lexicographically-sorted, comma-separated data lines that match the calculations from the raw log.  
       • Existence of /home/user/iot_gateway/reports/error_events.log containing precisely the lines from the raw 
         log whose status_code is ≥ 400, in original order.  
       • Proper newline termination of both files (Unix line-feed only, no carriage returns).  

    4. Deliverables the agent must print to the console:  
       • The content of summary.csv.  
       • A blank line.  
       • The first five lines of error_events.log (use `head ‑n 5`).  
       • The tree view requested in step 2d.  

    Complete everything using only non-privileged user operations; do not invoke sudo.  
    The agent may use any shell utilities, awk, python, etc., but only the output files will be graded.

          2023-10-11T17:20:35Z device-005 DATA_UPLOAD 180 502
          2023-10-11T17:45:50Z device-002 DATA_UPLOAD 101 503
          ------------------------------------
